intent: Can you pull up the connection details and current status for our production Postgres cluster with ID a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890? I need to check its configuration before we onboard a new microservice.

Our on-call engineer flagged a potential issue with database cluster 98765432-10ab-cdef-1234-567890abcdef—can you fetch its current status and specs so we can confirm whether it's actually running low on resources?
Can you pull the daily bandwidth usage for our three production apps—checkout, inventory, and customer-portal—so I can check if we're approaching our CDN plan's threshold this month?
I need to compare bandwidth consumption across all our regional apps for March 15th to figure out which one caused the spike in our hosting bill.
We had a database failover in our production cluster—please reassign floating IP 203.0.113.42 to the standby droplet so traffic reroutes without changing DNS records.
Our droplet tied to floating IP 198.51.100.7 is being decommissioned tonight; unassign the IP from it first so we can attach it to the replacement server tomorrow.
Set up a new firewall for our production Kubernetes cluster that only allows inbound traffic on ports 80 and 443 from the load balancer subnet.
We're onboarding a new client environment and need a firewall created that restricts SSH access to just our office IP range and opens port 5432 for the database team's VPN.
Can you pull up the list of all backups we have stored for our production database droplet with ID 78912345? I need to verify our disaster recovery coverage before the audit next week.
Before we decommission the old staging server (droplet ID 55231087), check what backups currently exist for it so we know if we need to manually snapshot anything first.
Our database cluster's maintenance window is currently set during business hours and it's causing disruptions to our production traffic—can you move it to Saturdays at 2am instead?

We just finished migrating our EU customer base to a new region, so please update the maintenance schedule for cluster db-prod-eu-01 to run early Sunday mornings UTC instead of the current weekday slot.
Our finance team needs the PDF copy of invoice ID a3f9c2e1-7b44-4d6a-9f21-8e6b1c0d5a77 for this month's expense reconciliation—can you pull that down?
I need to forward last quarter's invoice to our vendor management system as a PDF attachment; can you grab the document for invoice a12b3c4d-5e6f-7890-abcd-ef1234567890?
Can you attach the volume vol-8837 to our new database server droplet before end of day so the migration team can start restoring the backup?
We need to resize the storage volume backing our staging environment from 100GB to 250GB without losing any data—can you kick that off now?
